channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Two explosions strike cargo vessel in Gulf off Iraq
item.description: Two explosions hit a cargo vessel about 40 nautical miles southeast of Iraqs Umm Qasr, with one confirmed as a drone attack. The incident raises concerns about maritime security in the Gulf amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Kenya protests US Ebola quarantine facility at military base
item.description: Hundreds protested in central Kenya against a US plan to set up an Ebola quarantine facility at a military base, after the High Court ordered a temporary suspension. The facility has drawn local opposition over safety concerns.

item.title: Crimea faces gasoline shortages after Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: Drivers in Russian-controlled Crimea are grappling with gasoline rationing after Ukrainian drone attacks disrupted road supplies across southeastern Ukraine, affecting fuel distribution in the occupied peninsula.

item.title: Hezbollah Agrees to US Ceasefire Proposal with Israel
item.description: Lebanon's US embassy confirms Hezbollah accepted a US proposal for reciprocal cessation of attacks. The agreement would halt Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs in exchange for Hezbollah stopping attacks against Israel, with the framework to expand to all Lebanese territories.

item.title: US-China hold maritime military safety talks in Hawaii
item.description: The first US-China military communication since the Xi-Trump summit discussed avoiding misunderstandings during encounters between forces. Both sides agreed on the importance of effective communication for front-line forces.

item.title: France bans Israeli officials from Eurosatory defence show
item.description: Israel's Defence Ministry announced France has banned Israeli officials from participating in the Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris. The move comes amid growing international pressure over Israel's military actions.

item.title: Turkey emerges as strategic winner from Iran war through ambiguity
item.description: An analysis examining how Turkey's strategic ambiguity and balanced approach to the Iran conflict has positioned it favorably, allowing it to maintain relations with multiple actors while advancing its regional interests.

item.title: UN agency blocks Trump official's appointment over US arrears
item.description: A UN agency has blocked the appointment of a Trump administration official due to US payment arrears, highlighting ongoing tensions between the United States and international organizations over funding disputes.

item.title: Appeals court rules Trump transgender troop ban illegal
item.description: A federal appeals court has ruled that the Trump administration's policy banning transgender troops from military service is illegal, marking another legal setback for the policy that has faced multiple court challenges.

item.title: India's Modi meets Myanmar's military government leader in New Delhi
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Myanmar's military government leader in New Delhi, signaling continued engagement with the junta despite international criticism of its human rights record.

item.title: Trump faces new inflation warning over energy prices
item.description: Rising energy prices triggered by the Iran conflict are creating new inflation challenges for the Trump administration, with implications for bond markets and government borrowing costs.

item.title: Kuwait hit by missile and drone attack as regional tensions escalate
item.description: Kuwait was struck by a combined missile and drone attack as regional tensions continue to escalate following the broader Iran conflict, raising concerns about the safety of Gulf states.

item.title: Ukraine makes territorial gains as Russian advances stall
item.description: Ukrainian forces have begun making territorial gains as Russia's monthly territorial advances slipped into negative territory for the first time since 2023, according to DeepState monitoring reports.

item.title: Iran warns northern Israel residents to evacuate if Beirut attacked
item.description: Iran has issued a warning for residents of northern Israel to evacuate in the event of an attack on Beirut, signaling potential escalation in the conflict's scope and civilian targeting.

item.title: Netherlands suspends return of Lebanese asylum seekers over security
item.description: The Netherlands has suspended the return of Lebanese asylum seekers due to security concerns in Lebanon, reflecting ongoing instability in the country amid regional tensions.

item.title: US strikes Iranian radar sites as conflict continues
item.description: US forces have struck Iranian radar sites as part of ongoing military operations in the region, with the conflict showing no signs of de-escalation despite diplomatic efforts.

item.title: Iran seeks limited US deal to relieve economic strain
item.description: Iran is exploring a limited deal with the United States to ease economic pressure and buy time, according to Reuters. The strategy aims to relieve sanctions strain while maintaining key nuclear capabilities.

item.title: Russia bans aviation fuel exports until November
item.description: Russia has banned aviation fuel exports until November 30 as Ukrainian strikes on refineries and energy infrastructure continue, forcing Moscow to prioritize domestic supply.

item.title: Myanmar explosion kills dozens in rebel-held village
item.description: An unexplained detonation of explosives stored by one of Myanmar's rebel armies has killed dozens, according to the militia controlling the village, as search for survivors continues amid widespread devastation.

item.title: BP starts commercial gas production in Azerbaijan's ACG field
item.description: BP has begun commercial non-associated gas production at Azerbaijan's ACG field, marking a significant expansion of the country's energy exports amid growing European demand for alternatives to Russian gas.

item.title: India GDP growth likely eased in January-March quarter
item.description: India's economic growth likely eased to 7.2% in the first quarter of 2026, according to a Reuters poll, as weakening external demand and softer industrial activity offset strong government spending and resilient agricultural growth.

item.title: Israel's Defence Ministry says France bans Israeli officials from defence show
item.description: Israel's Defence Ministry has stated that France banned Israeli officials from participating in the Eurosatory defense exhibition, a significant diplomatic move amid ongoing international pressure.

item.title: Analysis: Hormuz crisis could reshape global farming and fertilizer supply
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has revealed the waterway's critical importance for fertilizer shipments, potentially forcing a global rethink of agricultural supply chains and food security.

item.title: US clandestinely guides vessels through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US is secretly escorting vessels through the Strait of Hormuz as peace negotiations with Iran continue to drag on, with ships facing threats from Iranian drones if they don't obtain approval.
